sourceSections: Introduction to the AI Model Showdown
Interactive 3D Art Museum: A Vision Realized
Age of Empires Clone: The Ultimate 3D Game Test
Conclusion: Fable 5's Dominance and Cost Implications

sections.lead: This video compares Claude's Opus 48 and its new flagship model, Fable 5, by having them build three complex projects.

bullets.label: Models Tested
bullets.text: The comparison involves Claude's Opus 48 and its new flagship model, Fable 5, in a head-to-head showdown.

bullets.label: Projects
bullets.text: Both models were tasked with building an e-commerce store, an interactive 3D art museum, and an Age of Empires clone.

bullets.label: Test Rules
bullets.text: Strict rules included a single one-shot output with no revisions, deployed live on the internet.

bullets.label: Initial Impression
bullets.text: The initial impression suggests that the results between the two models were not even close.

sections.lead: Fable 5 clearly won the e-commerce challenge, delivering a more polished site faster and with superior UI/UX compared to Opus 48.

bullets.label: Project Goal
bullets.text: The task was to build a complete e-commerce site for a fictional candle brand, 'Slow Burn,' aiming for Shopify-level quality with 30 products.

bullets.label: Opus 48 Output
bullets.text: Opus 48 delivered a functional site with readable text but had UI/UX flaws, including poor candle placement, an unappealing CTA, and a terrible navbar.

bullets.label: Fable 5 Output
bullets.text: Fable 5 produced a significantly cleaner and more polished site with classic e-commerce elements, better buttons, and a superior filtering system.

bullets.label: Image Generation
bullets.text: Fable 5 demonstrated a smarter approach to prompting image generation for product photos, resulting in a more cohesive aesthetic.

bullets.label: Time & Cost
bullets.text: Fable 5 was 15 minutes faster (35 minutes total), using 188k output tokens for $36.84, while Opus 48 used 198k tokens for $21.41.

sections.lead: Fable 5 delivered an exceptionally clean and functional interactive 3D art museum, vastly outperforming Opus 48 in quality and functionality.

bullets.label: Project Goal
bullets.text: The ambitious project was to create an interactive 3D art history museum in the browser with a zoomable timeline, placards, and realistic 3D galleries from Wikimedia Commons.

bullets.label: Opus 48 Output
bullets.text: Opus 48 generated a zoomable color-coded timeline, but artist elements were scattered, overlapped, and crucially, its galleries were not clickable.

bullets.label: Fable 5 Output
bullets.text: Fable 5 delivered an exceptionally clean 3D museum with an astrological timeline, smooth zooming, and fully functional, highly realistic 3D galleries.

bullets.label: Self-Correction
bullets.text: Fable 5 showed advanced self-correction, identifying complex data parsing errors like 'Francis Bacon (artist)' and disliking a 'pitch black floor'.

bullets.label: Time & Cost
bullets.text: Fable 5 completed its output about 15 minutes faster; Opus 48 used 437k output tokens ($46), while Fable 5 used 280k output tokens ($64).

sections.lead: Fable 5 astonishingly created a playable, high-quality Age of Empires-style game, while Opus 48 produced a completely broken application.

bullets.label: Project Goal
bullets.text: The final task was to build a fully playable Age of Empires-style RTS game in the browser with a 3D world, units, attack mechanics, and realistic graphics.

bullets.label: Opus 48 Output
bullets.text: Opus 48 finished in 33 minutes but produced

bullets.label: Fable 5 Output
bullets.text: Fable 5 completed in 30 minutes, delivering an astonishing 'Empires of Dawn' game with graphics described as 'insane' and 'just as good as Age of Empires'.

bullets.label: Game Features
bullets.text: Fable 5's game featured detailed civilizations, navigable maps, enemies with armor, and appeared fully playable with building mechanics and resource management.

sections.lead: Fable 5 consistently outperformed Opus 48 across all three challenges, demonstrating superior quality, functionality, and often efficiency.

bullets.label: Performance
bullets.text: Fable 5 demonstrated superior speed, token efficiency (in two of three tasks), and significantly higher quality and functionality in its outputs.

bullets.label: Strengths
bullets.text: It excelled in UI/UX design, realistic 3D rendering, complex data handling, and delivering truly interactive experiences.

bullets.label: Opus Weaknesses
bullets.text: Opus 48 often produced functional but flawed or broken applications, whereas Fable 5 delivered complete and polished solutions.

bullets.label: Cost vs. Value
bullets.text: While Fable 5's usage-based pricing is higher, the substantial leap in quality and capability it offers makes it a compelling choice.

bullets.label: Overall Dominance
bullets.text: This comparison solidifies Fable 5's position as a powerful, next-generation AI model for code generation.
